headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical. Canonical published composite is 0.0 (rank 188, all dimensions at 1) — the rotation-state composite of 6.3 is stale and does not match canonical countries.json. Per floor protocol composite cannot move below floor; Russia is floor-designated and the May 9 conduct adds to the floor-conduct record without composite movement. New conduct documented on day 1 of US-brokered trilateral ceasefire: (1) Drone strike on Kharkiv residential high-rise on the evening of May 9, day 1 of the ceasefire (5 injured including two 8-year-old boys with acute stress reactions) = direct breach of stated ceasefire commitment; (2) Defense Ministry claim of 'strict observance' simultaneous with confirmed front-line offensive operations on advancing sectors = active misrepresentation under counterparty record; (3) Victory Day parade scaled down (no heavy weapons, Putin: 'military needs them at the front') — PR management of ceasefire optics while continuing strikes; (4) North Korean troops integrated into parade for the first time — structural SYS signal of DPRK military partnership institutionalization; (5) Putin framed war as Russia fighting 'NATO-backed aggressive force' — sustained external-threat rhetorical escalation in BND. Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule (May 6/7 precedent) and floor protocol: ceasefire violations register against Russia as counterparty; floor sustained.
rotation_state_data_integrity_flag: Rotation-state.json shows Russia composite 6.3 / Critical but canonical countries.json shows 0.0 / Critical (all dimensions at 1). Per hard constraint, canonical value used (0.0). The 6.3 in rotation-state is stale — likely pre-floor-designation artifact. Sync action required separate from this proposal. Logged for data-team. Floor-protocol scoring rules apply regardless.
key_evidence: May 9 2026 evening (in-window): Russian drone strike on Kharkiv residential high-rise — 5 injured including two 8-year-olds with acute stress reactions. Direct breach of day 1 of US-brokered trilateral ceasefire.

May 9 2026 (in-window): Russia's Defense Ministry claimed 'strict observance' of ceasefire while simultaneously conducting offensive operations on advancing front-line sectors and the Kharkiv strike — active misrepresentation pattern.
May 9 2026 (in-window): Russian bombs, drones, artillery struck civilian Kharkiv and Kherson regions; at least 2 killed, 7 wounded including a 14-year-old boy.
May 9 2026 (in-window): Putin Victory Day speech framed Russia as fighting 'NATO-backed aggressive force' — sustained external-threat rhetorical posture; declared war 'coming to an end.'
May 9 2026 (in-window): Victory Day parade scaled down — no heavy weapons (Putin: 'needed at the front'). PR management of ceasefire optics while continuing strikes.

May 9 2026 (in-window): North Korean troops integrated into Victory Day parade for the FIRST TIME — public display of institutionalized DPRK military partnership. New SYS conduct category: state-sponsor military-coalition expansion.

Cumulative floor-conduct record: established pattern of civilian targeting in Ukraine, mass detention of Ukrainian civilians, deportation of children, INGO restrictions, suppression of internal anti-war voices.

evidence.source: PBS Newshour — Moscow holds scaled-back Victory Day parade
evidence.finding: Scaled-down parade without heavy weapons; Putin 'needed at the front' framing; PR management of ceasefire optics.
Putin declared war 'coming to an end' while authorising continued offensive operations; NATO-threat framing.

evidence.source: NBC News — Putin hosts scaled-back military parade
evidence.finding: Documented internet shutdowns in parade environment; controlled-information posture sustained.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Russia holds downsized Victory Day
evidence.finding: North Korean troops integrated into parade for the first time — public DPRK partnership institutionalization.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Russia-Ukraine trade fire despite Victory Day ceasefire
evidence.finding: Kharkiv residential strike confirmed; Russia continued ceasefire-day strikes while claiming observance.

evidence.source: Critical Threats — Russian offensive campaign assessment May 9
evidence.finding: Russian forces continued offensive operations on multiple advancing sectors during ceasefire day 1.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). Active suppression of independent monitoring; documented civilian-harm response only via state-controlled narrative.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Continued targeting of civilian residential buildings (Kharkiv) within hours of ceasefire start; affective regard absent.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Active offensive operations on day 1 of agreed ceasefire; harm-causing action sustained, not reduced to zero.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). DPRK military-partnership institutionalization narrows protected-population scope; LGBT and political-dissident criminalization patterns sustained.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). NATO-threat rhetorical escalation; ceasefire-claim vs. action contradiction; B3 scope-clarity actively violated by 'strict observance' false claim.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). Defense Ministry claimed 'strict observance' simultaneous with documented violations; AB1 harm-acknowledgment floor active.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY: institutionalization of DPRK military partnership publicly displayed for first time — structural state-sponsor military-coalition expansion. Floor sustained; conduct appended to record.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Declared ceasefire vs. day-1 documented breaches = bad-faith-ceasefire-rule confirmation cycle 4 (May 5, May 7, and now May 9 partial-compliance variant).

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. Composite cannot move below floor. New conduct documented for floor record.

trilateral_methodology_call: TRILATERAL FORMAT DOES NOT ALTER RUSSIA'S FLOOR ATTRIBUTION. Russia is the documented counterparty in the bad-faith-ceasefire rule applied May 5 and May 7. The May 9 trilateral was an opportunity for Russia to demonstrate partial compliance — and partial compliance WAS documented (Zelenskyy acknowledged reduced large-scale bombardments) — but the Kharkiv residential drone strike on day 1 plus continued front-line offensive operations and Defense Ministry false 'strict observance' claim constitute direct breach evidence. For floor-designated entities, partial compliance does not generate composite movement (floor cannot move). The trilateral methodology call (Ukraine cycle) applies symmetric attribution: Russia's day-1 breaches register as counterparty cost. Floor-conduct record updated; composite unchanged.
new_conduct_categories_notated: Day 1 trilateral-ceasefire breach: Kharkiv residential drone strike with civilian casualties including minors (May 9 evening)
Active misrepresentation: Defense Ministry 'strict observance' claim simultaneous with documented strikes
DPRK military-partnership institutionalization: North Korean troops publicly integrated into Victory Day parade for the first time
Sustained NATO-threat rhetorical escalation: Putin Victory Day speech framing of war as fighting 'NATO-backed aggressive force'
Scaled-down parade as PR management of ceasefire optics while continuing offensive operations
negativeSignals: Day-1 ceasefire breach with civilian casualties including minors; Defense Ministry active misrepresentation; DPRK partnership institutionalization on public display; NATO-threat rhetorical escalation; sustained offensive operations on advancing sectors; controlled-information posture (internet shutdowns); bad-faith-ceasefire-rule fourth confirmation cycle.
positiveSignals: Partial compliance acknowledged by Ukrainian counterparty (Zelenskyy: 'reduced large-scale bombardments') — insufficient to move floor; 1,000-prisoner-exchange preparation = cooperative-action evidence credited to bilateral system level, not Russia alone; Victory Day parade scaled down reduces some immediate symbolic harm but does not offset breach pattern.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Russia's floor placement is reinforced by the fourth confirmation cycle of the bad-faith-ceasefire rule. The DPRK partnership institutionalization is a new SYS conduct category that may affect adjacent assessments (North Korea, China-Russia relationship structures). Cross-reference: Ukraine (paired counterparty, +50.0 functional sustained), Belarus (counterparty-adjacent), North Korea (institutionalized partnership-adjacent).

DPRK partnership institutionalization documented as new SYS conduct category
